---
title: "Homework"
subtitle: "STAT 184 — Summer 2026"
---

There will be 1-2 homework assignments per week in the course. The lowest score is dropped. The following is an outline of the first two homeworks:

::: {tbl-colwidths="[5, 25, 15, 15, 25, 15]"}
| # | Topic | Out | Due | Files | Canvas Link |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | Setup Confirmation (R, RStudio, Quarto installed) | Mon May 18 | Wed May 20 | [Quarto](hw0_template.qmd){download=""} · [HTML](hw0_template.html) · [PDF](hw0_template.pdf) | [Click here](https://psu.instructure.com/courses/2487468/assignments/18308171) |
| 1 | Basic R & Plots | Mon May 18 | Fri May 22 | [Quarto](hw1_template.qmd){download=""} · [HTML](hw1_template.html) · [PDF](hw1_template.pdf) | [Click here](https://psu.instructure.com/courses/2487468/assignments/18311694) |
:::

## How to submit

1. Work in Quarto, render to PDF or HTML.
2. Include a code appendix at the end (for HW 2+).
3. Include the **AI use note** (1–2 points).
4. Submit the rendered file to Canvas.

## AI policy

You may use AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, Copilot, etc.) on homework. You must:

- Read everything you submit. You own the output.
- Include an AI use note (one sentence is fine).
- Be prepared to answer questions about your code if I ask.

See the [Academic Integrity & AI Policy](../../syllabus/academic-integrity-and-ai-policy.qmd) for the full version.

## Late policy

Email me for an extension if you need one. Extensions are generally 24–48 hours. Submissions more than 48 hours late without prior arrangement may not be accepted. Lowest score is dropped.

## Standard AI Use Note

Every homework rubric includes the following (1–2 points):

> # AI Disclosure
> 
> *Check off all boxes that apply to your usage and provide a brief description of how the tool was utilized.*
> 
> - [ ] No AI used
> - [ ] Syntax / Debugging (e.g., finding a missing comma, fixing an error code)
> - [ ] Conceptual explanation (e.g., asking how a `left_join` works)
> - [ ] Code Generation (e.g., asking AI to write a function from scratch)
> 
> *Briefly describe your use (1-2 sentences):* [Type your explanation here]

## Template

A starter Quarto template is in the [reference materials](../../reference/hw-template.qmd).
